claim: Top 1% venture capital exits have grown from $10B (2020-2024) to $32B (early 2026), representing a 3.2x increase in under 24 months
actionable: Track exit valuations quarterly to anticipate market shifts in AI company value capture
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Less than 5% of enterprise workforce has adopted AI for real work despite Anthropic and OpenAI adding more monthly revenue than hyperscalers
actionable: Target the 95% of uninformed enterprises with AI solutions; there is massive beachhead opportunity before saturation
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Native AI companies operate with lean, aggressive teams and are building multiple specialized agents instead of rigid organizational structures
actionable: Adopt lightweight team structures and focus on building agent swarms for your company operations instead of hiring traditional roles
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Being in the token path (required for accessing frontier AI capabilities) is now the number one criterion for venture investment decisions
actionable: Ensure your AI product directly consumes tokens from frontier models; products not in the token path face pricing pressure from competition
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: 40% of AI50 startups (per Forbes) drop off annually, indicating an incredibly short half-life for AI companies compared to previous tech cycles
actionable: Plan for rapid obsolescence of your AI product; build with assumption that your competitive moat will erode every 12 months
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Chinese AI models are 6 months behind the US frontier but 10x cheaper, creating a classic innovator's dilemma where 80% of tasks can be done with cheaper models
actionable: Expect margin compression as second-tier models improve; build products that require frontier intelligence rather than competing on cost
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Early stage venture loss ratios should be 60% (industry standard), not single digits; low loss ratios indicate insufficient risk-taking
actionable: If your AI startup portfolio has sub-10% loss ratios, you are not taking enough risk; increase allocation to moonshot bets
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show

claim: Supply constraints (data center capacity unavailable until late 2028-early 2029) make bubble conditions unlikely in the next 3 years
actionable: Invest confidently in AI infrastructure and foundational models; supply scarcity protects valuations through 2028
source_title: The New Rule for Picking AI Winners | The a16z Show
